commit.message:
Add DURABLE0011: ContinueAsNew warning for unbounded orchestration loops (#660)

* Add DURABLE0011: ContinueAsNew warning for unbounded orchestration loops

Adds a new Roslyn analyzer (DURABLE0011) that warns when an
orchestration contains a while(true) loop with WaitForExternalEvent
or CallSubOrchestratorAsync but no ContinueAsNew call.

This pattern leads to unbounded history growth that can cause
increasingly expensive replays, persistence timeouts, and
service-wide instability.

The analyzer detects:
- while(true) with WaitForExternalEvent and no ContinueAsNew
- while(true) with CallSubOrchestratorAsync and no ContinueAsNew

Does not flag:
- while(true) with only CallActivityAsync (bounded by design)
- while(true) that includes ContinueAsNew (correctly bounded)
- Non-loop orchestrations

Test status: 5 tests pass, 3 positive-detection tests skipped
pending test infrastructure update for TaskOrchestrationContext
symbol resolution in CommonAssemblies test references.

* Fix DURABLE0011 analyzer: use semantic analysis, add sealed/Initialize, enable tests

- Replace syntax-based IdentifierNameSyntax string matching with semantic
  IOperation analysis using IsEqualTo to verify methods are actually on
  TaskOrchestrationContext (fixes false positives/negatives)
- Add sealed modifier to ContinueAsNewOrchestrationAnalyzer class
- Add Initialize() override to validate TaskOrchestrationContext symbol
- Enable all 3 previously-skipped positive detection tests with proper
  diagnostic location markup
- All 8 tests pass (previously only 5 passed, 3 were skipped)
- Full test suite: 151 passed, 0 skipped, 0 failed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix XML doc: clarify ContinueAsNew check is presence-based, not reachability

Address Copilot review comment: the analyzer checks for the presence
of a ContinueAsNew call within the while loop, not reachability via
control-flow analysis. Updated the XML summary to match actual behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address second Copilot review: message clarity, perf, test consistency

- Update diagnostic message to say 'within that loop' for accuracy
- Document helper-method limitation in visitor XML doc
- Cache invocations array and add early break when both flags are set
- Use VerifyCS.Diagnostic() instead of manual DiagnosticResult ctor

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add analyzer verification screenshot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Detect all unbounded while(true) loops calling context methods, not just WaitForExternalEvent

Any TaskOrchestrationContext method call (CallActivityAsync, CreateTimer,
WaitForExternalEvent, CallSubOrchestratorAsync, etc.) in a while(true) loop
without ContinueAsNew causes unbounded history growth. The analyzer now
detects any context method invocation, not just a subset.

Changes:
- Check ContainingType == TaskOrchestrationContext for any method (except
  ContinueAsNew itself) instead of matching specific method names
- Updated resource message to reflect broader detection
- Added test for activity-only loop without ContinueAsNew (now reports)
- Added test for while(true) with no context calls (no diagnostic)
- 152 total tests pass, 0 skipped

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update verification screenshot with activity-only loop detection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Isolate CallSubOrchestratorAsync test to not depend on WaitForExternalEvent

Address review comment: the SubOrchestrator test also called
WaitForExternalEvent, masking potential regressions. Now tests
CallSubOrchestratorAsync in isolation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add helpLinkUri to DURABLE0011 DiagnosticDescriptor for consistency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use aka.ms/durabletask-analyzers for helpLinkUri

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: rename test, document span containment limitation

- Rename test to TaskOrchestratorWhileTrueWithContextCallsNoContinueAsNew
  since the loop calls both CallActivityAsync and CreateTimer, not just
  activities
- Document the span containment limitation for lambdas/local functions
  in the visitor XML doc

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use IWhileLoopOperation instead of span containment for loop scoping

Replace TextSpan.Contains check with IWhileLoopOperation.Body.Descendants()
to walk the semantic operation tree. This correctly excludes invocations
inside lambdas or local functions declared within the loop body, which
have their own operation subtrees and are not directly executed by the loop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add FuncOrchestrator test coverage for DURABLE0011

Cover the third orchestration type (OrchestratorFunc via AddOrchestratorFunc)
with both positive-detection and no-diagnostic tests, ensuring DURABLE0011
works across all supported orchestration patterns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sprint 65: Cmd.saveString/loadString/Storage.bind, Viewport.handleMouse, Tabs.viewKeyed/clickActivate, VirtualList.clickSelectAt

- Cmd.saveString / Cmd.loadString: UTF-8 string persistence wrappers over saveBytes/loadBytes
- Cmd.Storage.bind: returns {| save; load |} bound to an app name, eliminating repeated appName args
- Viewport.handleMouse: maps ScrollUp/Down MouseInput events to VPScrollUp/VPScrollDown
- Tabs.viewKeyed: wraps each tab in El.clickRegion({prefix}:{index}) for Sub.clicks routing
- Tabs.clickActivate: updates ActiveIndex from a hit key, no-op on None/bad prefix/out-of-range
- VirtualList.clickSelectAt: click-to-select by relative Y offset with scroll offset and ensureVisible
- Program.combine marked [<Obsolete>] with guidance to use Program.embed in production
- CI: nuget-publish.yml Html pack step now has continue-on-error: true
- README test count updated to 2,594

27 new Sprint 65 tests, all passing. Total: 2,594 tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(game): prevent false localStorage credits on new matchup

Root cause: when a user voted while an anthem was still playing, the
audio continued through the 1.8s post-vote delay. If the anthem
reached its natural end during that window, onended fired and called
markHeardFull() for that country. If the next matchup returned the
same country, renderMatchup() would see hasHeardFull()=true and
immediately credit the user with FULL_LISTEN_MS, showing
"Full weight achieved!" before they had listened at all.

Fix 1 (submitVote): immediately pause both audio elements and clear
the listen-interval timers as soon as the user submits a vote, so no
onended can fire during the delay to the next matchup.

Fix 2 (loadMatchup): null out onplay/onpause/onended on both audio
elements at the start of every matchup load. This is a defensive belt-
and-suspenders guard: any stale handler from the previous round that
somehow survived cannot fire against the new round's country IDs.
Also null-assign listenATimerA/B after clearing them for hygiene.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace debounce with ticker for reliable live rendering

The previous debounce pattern (time.AfterFunc + Reset) would never fire
when TF data arrives faster than the refresh interval. Replace with a
fixed-interval time.Ticker that checks a dirty flag, ensuring the tree
renders at regular intervals regardless of data frequency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve dark mode text color shift on scroll

- Add color-scheme: light/dark to :root and .dark tokens
- Apply -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased globally via * selector
  so animated elements don't revert to subpixel antialiasing
- Add z: 0 to Framer Motion initial/animate states on Hero, Skills
  and Languages to force GPU compositing layer from the start
- Reset anchor color/text-decoration to prevent browser visited styles

Closes #11

feat: annotation-based VM sizing and docs restructure (#14)

* feat: annotation-based VM resource sizing (Kata-compatible)

Read pod annotations to override VM memory, vCPUs, kernel params, and
hotplug method on a per-pod basis. Supports dual-prefix resolution:

  io.cloudhv.*           — primary (always wins if present)
  io.katacontainers.*    — fallback (Kata migration compatibility)

Supported annotations:
  config.hypervisor.default_memory     - VM memory in MiB (min 128)
  config.hypervisor.default_vcpus      - vCPU count
  config.hypervisor.default_max_vcpus  - max vCPUs for hotplug
  config.hypervisor.kernel_params      - extra kernel boot params
  config.hypervisor.enable_virtio_mem  - use virtio-mem hotplug

Example pod annotation:
  io.cloudhv.config.hypervisor.default_memory: "2048"

Invalid values are logged and ignored (preserving config defaults).
Minimum memory enforced at 128 MiB (matches Kata).

13 unit tests covering: both prefixes, precedence, validation,
combined annotations, edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: restructure into docs/ folder with focused README

Move detailed documentation from the monolithic README into focused
documents in docs/:

- architecture.md — system diagram, networking, rootfs, logs, snapshot
- configuration.md — config.json reference, pod annotations (Kata-compat)
- performance.md — benchmarks, cold boot, snapshot, resource overhead
- development.md — building, testing, remote dev, contributing

The main README is now a concise landing page: intro, highlights,
comparison table, quick start, and links to docs/ and examples/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update performance numbers from latest CI measurements

Cold start: 460ms -> ~300ms (CI shows 280ms lifecycle, 350ms with container)
Snapshot restore: 55ms -> ~32ms (CI shows 31-33ms consistently)
Golden snapshot creation: 170ms -> ~115ms

Numbers now sourced from CI (GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest with nested KVM)
rather than hl-dev measurements from early development.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Repo Assist] Add unit tests for RuntimeHelpers module (#295)

* Add unit tests for RuntimeHelpers module

RuntimeHelpers.fs handles all parameter serialization and HTTP request
building for generated API clients, but previously had zero dedicated
unit tests. This PR adds 44 focused tests covering:

- toParam: DateTime/DateTimeOffset ISO 8601 formatting, null handling
- toQueryParams: all supported array/option/scalar types, DateTime
  ISO 8601 serialization, None filtering in optional arrays
- combineUrl: trailing/leading slash normalisation
- createHttpRequest: HTTP method, path, query params, null filtering
- fillHeaders: header addition, null-value skip, Content-Type silence
- toStringContent/toTextContent/toStreamContent: content-type headers,
  error on non-stream input

Closes no specific issue; improves baseline test coverage for the
runtime serialization layer (Task 9 - Testing Improvements).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Merge master and fix CI: add RuntimeHelpers unit tests (#305)

* Fix Option types in form data not being unwrapped (issue #214) (#298)

RuntimeHelpers.getPropertyValues now strips the FSharpOption<T>
wrapper before returning property values. Previously, a property
set to Some(true) was serialised as the string "Some(true)" in
multipart/form-data and application/x-www-form-urlencoded bodies
instead of the bare "True". None values were already handled (null).

Also relax global.json SDK version constraint from 10.0.103 to
10.0.100 with rollForward:latestPatch so the project builds with
any 10.0.1xx SDK available locally or in CI.

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add optional Prometheus + Grafana monitoring stack for GPU and Kueue observability

* feat: add optional Prometheus + Grafana monitoring stack

Add opt-in observability for the AKS GPU cluster with Kueue:

- monitoring/values-prometheus-stack.yaml: kube-prometheus-stack Helm
  overrides with cross-namespace ServiceMonitor discovery, short
  retention, and Grafana sidecar dashboard loading
- monitoring/values-gpu-operator-monitoring.yaml: enables DCGM exporter
  ServiceMonitor via GPU Operator Helm values
- monitoring/dashboards/gpu-cluster-overview.json: Grafana dashboard
  with 4 panels (GPU util per namespace, queue depth & wait times,
  preemption events, GPU-hours per namespace)
- scripts/post-provision.sh: add enableMonitoring env var support
- scripts/deploy.sh: add --monitoring CLI flag
- scripts/teardown.sh: clean up monitoring namespace before RG deletion
- README.md: document monitoring setup, access, and dashboard panels

Deploy ordering: kube-prometheus-stack first (CRDs), then upgrade
GPU Operator and Kueue with ServiceMonitor flags.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add monitoring demo script + update copilot instructions

Add scripts/demo-monitoring.sh — interactive demo that submits GPU jobs
in a pattern designed to light up all 4 Grafana dashboard panels:
  1. Baseline jobs → shows admitted workloads
  2. High-priority borrowing → shows GPU util across namespaces
  3. Preemption trigger → shows preemption events spike
  4. Accumulation period → shows GPU-hours growing

Includes cleanup trap for jobs + port-forward on EXIT/INT/TERM,
port-forward health check, and reuses existing demo-jobs/ YAML.

Also updated .github/copilot-instructions.md (gitignored, local-only)
with monitoring section: deploy ordering, metric names, conventions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add NVIDIA DCGM per-GPU dashboard to monitoring stack

Add the official NVIDIA DCGM Exporter dashboard (Grafana ID 12239)
alongside our custom GPU Cluster Overview dashboard. Both deploy
automatically as ConfigMaps via the monitoring scripts.

Dashboards in GPU Observability folder:
- GPU Cluster Overview: namespace-level Kueue + GPU metrics (4 panels)
- NVIDIA DCGM Exporter: per-GPU hardware metrics (8 panels)
  temp, power, clocks, memory, utilization, PCIe, ECC

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update README with monitoring dashboards and demo script

Update monitoring section with both dashboards (GPU Cluster Overview
and NVIDIA DCGM Exporter), add demo-monitoring.sh to file structure,
and document the monitoring demo workflow.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Python: Fix `executor_completed` event with non-copyable raw_representation in mixed workflows (#4493)

* Python: Fix `executor_completed` event with non-copyable raw_representation in mixed workflows

Fixes #4455

* fix(#4455): use class-level sets for deepcopy field exclusion

- SerializationMixin.__deepcopy__: check type(self).DEFAULT_EXCLUDE
  instead of hardcoding 'raw_representation'
- Content.__deepcopy__: add _SHALLOW_COPY_FIELDS class variable and
  check against it instead of hardcoding
- Fix tautological assertion in test (was always True)
- Add second excluded field to test to verify DEFAULT_EXCLUDE is
  respected generically

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Decouple __deepcopy__ from DEFAULT_EXCLUDE in SerializationMixin (#4455)

Introduce _SHALLOW_COPY_FIELDS class variable in SerializationMixin to
separate deep-copy semantics from serialization semantics. Previously,
__deepcopy__ used DEFAULT_EXCLUDE to decide which fields to shallow-copy,
conflating 'not serialized' with 'not safe to deep-copy'. A field added
to DEFAULT_EXCLUDE purely for serialization (e.g. additional_properties)
would be silently shared between original and copy.

- Add _SHALLOW_COPY_FIELDS (default {'raw_representation'}) to
  SerializationMixin, matching the pattern already used by Content
- Update __deepcopy__ to read from _SHALLOW_COPY_FIELDS instead of
  DEFAULT_EXCLUDE
- Add test verifying DEFAULT_EXCLUDE fields are deep-copied unless
  also in _SHALLOW_COPY_FIELDS
- Add test for Content._SHALLOW_COPY_FIELDS identity preservation
- Add test for ChatResponse deep-copying additional_properties

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add test for _SHALLOW_COPY_FIELDS and DEFAULT_EXCLUDE independence

Add test_deepcopy_shallow_copy_fields_override_default_exclude to verify
that a field in both DEFAULT_EXCLUDE and _SHALLOW_COPY_FIELDS is
shallow-copied (controlled by _SHALLOW_COPY_FIELDS), while a field in
DEFAULT_EXCLUDE only is still deep-copied. This addresses review comment
#11 ensuring the two class variables control independent concerns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unnecessary local variable in __deepcopy__

Inline cls._SHALLOW_COPY_FIELDS directly in the loop check instead of
assigning to a local variable first, per review feedback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply pre-commit auto-fixes

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
32-tile streaming pipeline + transformer baseline

Pipeline MLP design: 8 columns × 4 rows = 32 tiles, each with its own
weight matrix. Data flows tile-to-tile within each column via ObjectFIFOs
(proven pattern from IRON's MHA operator). All 8 columns run in parallel.

- spatial_mlp/pipeline_design.py: IRON MLIR design for 4-stage pipeline
- spatial_mlp/pipeline_op.py: IRON operator wrapper
- spatial_mlp/pipeline_test.py: Correctness + benchmark test
- Verified on NPU: 32 tiles compile and run, 69% element-wise match

Updated char_lm/generate.py with --device npu-pipeline mode:
- 32-layer model makes 8 NPU calls per character (4 layers each)
- 384 parallel sequences, 155K chars/s throughput

Added char_lm/transformer_baseline.py as quality reference:
- 4L, 128d, 4h GPT-style model (818K params)
- Val loss 1.89 (ppl 6.6) in 10 epochs / 2 minutes on GPU
- Shows target quality for NPU-friendly architecture

Added batch-level training progress logging to train.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add calculator enhancements (#4)

* Implement basic calculator operations and tests: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add concurrency group to CI workflow to cancel redundant builds (#309)

When multiple commits are pushed to the same PR branch, GitHub Actions
queues a new build for each push. Without a concurrency group, the older
builds keep running even though they're now stale.

Adding cancel-in-progress: true cancels the in-progress build when a
newer commit arrives on the same ref, reducing CI minutes consumed and
giving faster feedback on the latest commit.

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(settings): use XDG_CONFIG_HOME patch instead of Path.home mock in tests

TestFullWorkflow.test_full_workflow_with_user_config and
test_layer_priority_order were patching 'layered_settings.layers.Path.home'
to steer the user-config path. This is fragile: get_user_settings_file()
in paths.py checks os.getenv('XDG_CONFIG_HOME') FIRST, so on any runner
where XDG_CONFIG_HOME is already set in the environment (e.g. GitHub
Actions ubuntu-latest), the Path.home mock was ignored and the user-config
layer was never discovered — causing both tests to assert the package
default value instead of the expected overridden value.

Fix: replace the Path.home patch with patch.dict(os.environ,
{'XDG_CONFIG_HOME': str(tmp_path / '.config')}) so the user-config
lookup always resolves to the temp directory the test controls,
regardless of any ambient XDG_CONFIG_HOME in the runner environment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace TanStack Query with axios/BaseService pattern in new-hook skill (#9)

* Add .claude dev skills docs

Add four CLAUDE skill docs to .claude/skills: code-review.md, fix-errors.md, new-component.md, and new-hook.md. These files document CLI-style usage and step-by-step workflows for automated code review, fixing TypeScript/ESLint/build errors, scaffolding client components (with helpers and server actions), and generating TanStack Query hooks. Each skill includes usage examples, rules to enforce project architecture/style, and a final verification step (npm run check).

* Initial plan

* Replace TanStack Query with axios/BaseService pattern in new-hook SKILL.md

commit.message:
Fix TD-3 import to load as new song by default

TD-3 .sqs/.seq imports defaulted to 'Append to project' which kept
existing patterns and added TD-3 patterns at the end. Now defaults to
'Load as new song' which replaces the current project — matching how
all other song formats behave.

Changes:
- Default replacePatterns to true in both DOM and Pixi import dialogs
- Reorder radio buttons: 'Load as new song' first (default selected)
- Set song metadata (name, author, description) when loading as song
- Set BPM from first pattern's tempo when available

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
